You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: b139c14d).

## Issue to Resolve

Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE - https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#non-constant_names
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val someMap: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Observed Behavior
ktlint require SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val SOME_MAP: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Create object, with mutable collection property. 

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.50.0

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

You're right. Can you provide a little more context where this variable is declared? Is it at top level, or in a companion object or class?
I have edited the issue, so you can see where the property is declared. 

In my case it is an object's property. I did not check for other cases as top level or companion object's variables (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming should not be applied to class properties according to https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names).

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

Let me start by understanding the issue. The problem is that ktlint is requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for mutable collection properties in objects, but according to Android's Kotlin style guide, mutable collection properties should NOT use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GET_KEYWORD
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 25→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 26→ */
 27→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 28→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 29→    Rule.Experimental {
 30→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 31→        node: ASTNode,
 32→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 33→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 34→    ) {
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 37→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    private fun visitProperty(
 41→        property: ASTNode,
 42→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 43→    ) {
 44→        property
 45→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 46→            ?.let { identifier ->
 47→                when {
 48→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 49→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 50→                    }
 51→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 52→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 53→                    }
 54→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 55→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 56→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 57→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 58→                    }
 59→                    else -> {
 60→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 61→                    }
 62→                }
 63→            }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 67→        identifier: ASTNode,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        identifier
 71→            .text
 72→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 73→            ?.let {
 74→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 75→            }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 79→        identifier: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        identifier
 83→            .text
 84→            .takeUnless {
 85→                // Allow
 86→                // object Foo {
 87→                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 88→                // }
 89→                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 90→            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 91→            ?.let {
 92→                emit(
 93→                    identifier.startOffset,
 94→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 95→                    false,
 96→                )
 97→            }
 98→    }
 99→
100→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
101→        identifier: ASTNode,
102→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
103→    ) {
104→        identifier
105→            .text
106→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
107→            ?.let {
108→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
109→            }
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
113→
114→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
117→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
118→            ?.children()
119→            .orEmpty()
120→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
123→
124→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
127→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
128→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
129→            containsValKeyword() &&
130→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
133→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
134→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
135→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
136→            ?.let { identifier ->
137→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
138→            }
139→            ?: false
140→
141→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
142→        treeParent
143→            .children()
144→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
145→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
146→            .any { it.text == identifier }
147→
148→    private companion object {
149→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
150→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
151→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
152→        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
153→    }
154→}
155→
156→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId
157→

1→!!! important
   2→    Up and until Ktlint version `0.47`, experimental were located in a separate `experimental` rule set. As of Ktlint version `0.48`, each rule set can optionally define experimental rules.
   3→
   4→All experimental rules described below are part of the `standard` rule set of Ktlint. To enable all experimental rules (from all rule sets), set `editorconfig` property below:
   5→```ini
   6→[*.{kt,kts}]
   7→ktlint_experimental=enabled
   8→```
   9→Also see [enable/disable specific rules](../configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules).
  10→
  11→## Binary expression wrapping
  12→
  13→Wraps binary expression at the operator reference whenever the binary expression does not fit on the line. In case the binary expression is nested, the expression is evaluated from outside to inside. If the left and right hand sides of the binary expression, after wrapping, fit on a single line then the inner binary expressions will not be wrapped. If one or both inner binary expression still do not fit on a single after wrapping of the outer binary expression, then each of those inner binary expressions will be wrapped.
  14→
  15→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
  16→
  17→    ```kotlin
  18→    fun foo() {
  19→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
  20→        // at the X character on the right                       X
  21→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) ||
  22→            (leftHandSideLongExpression &&
  23→                rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
  24→            // do something
  25→        }
  26→    }
  27→    ```
  28→
  29→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  30→
  31→    ```kotlin
  32→    fun foo() {
  33→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
  34→        // at the X character on the right                       X
  35→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) || (leftHandSideLongExpression && rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
  36→            // do something
  37→        }
  38→    }
  39→    ```
  40→
  41→Rule id: `binary-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
  42→
  43→## Blank line before declarations
  44→
  45→Requires a blank line before any class or function declaration. No blank line is required between the class signature and the first declaration in the class. In a similar way, a blank line is required before any list of top level or class properties. No blank line is required before local properties or between consecutive properties.
  46→
  47→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
  48→
  49→    ```kotlin
  50→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
  51→
  52→    class FooBar {
  53→        val foo2 = "foo2"
  54→        val foo3 = "foo3"
  55→
  56→        fun bar1() {
  57→           val foo4 = "foo4"
  58→           val foo5 = "foo5"
  59→        }
  60→
  61→        fun bar2() = "bar"
  62→
  63→        val foo6 = "foo3"
  64→        val foo7 = "foo4"
  65→
  66→        enum class Foo {}
  67→    }
  68→    ```
  69→
  70→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  71→
  72→    ```kotlin
  73→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
  74→    class FooBar {
  75→        val foo2 = "foo2"
  76→        val foo3 = "foo3"
  77→        fun bar1() {
  78→           val foo4 = "foo4"
  79→           val foo5 = "foo5"
  80→        }
  81→        fun bar2() = "bar"
  82→        val foo6 = "foo3"
  83→        val foo7 = "foo4"
  84→        enum class Foo {}
  85→    }
  86→    ```
  87→
  88→Rule id: `blank-line-before-declaration` (`standard` rule set)
  89→
  90→!!! Note
  91→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
  92→
  93→## Class signature
  94→
  95→Rewrites the class signature to a consistent format respecting the `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` if set. In the `ktlint_official` code style all class parameters are wrapped by default. Set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_class_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters` to a value greater than 1 to allow class with a few parameters to be placed on a single line.
  96→The other code styles allow an infinite amount of parameters on the same line (as long as the `max_line_length` is not exceeded) unless `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_class_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters` is set explicitly.
  97→
  98→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint (ktlint_official)"
  99→

 104→        a: Any
 105→    )
 106→    class Foo2(

 108→        b: Any
 109→    )

 270→## Discouraged comment location
 271→
 272→Detect discouraged comment locations (no autocorrect).
 273→
 274→!!! note
 275→    Kotlin allows comments to be placed almost everywhere. As this can lead to code which is hard to read, most of them will never be used in practice. Ideally each rule takes comments at all possible locations into account. Sometimes this is really hard and not worth the effort. By explicitly forbidding such comment locations, the development of those rules becomes a bit easier. 
 276→
 277→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 278→
 279→    ```kotlin
 280→    fun <T> /* some comment */ foo(t: T) = "some-result"
 281→
 282→    fun foo() {
 283→        if (true)
 284→            // some comment
 285→            bar()
 286→    }
 287→    ```

 289→Rule id: `discouraged-comment-location` (`standard` rule set)

 291→## Disallow empty lines at start of class body
 292→
 293→Detect blank lines at start of a class body.
 294→
 295→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 296→

 298→    class Foo {

 300→    }
 301→    ```

 304→

 307→
 308→        val foo = "foo"
 309→    }
 310→    ```

 313→
 314→!!! Note
 315→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 316→
 317→## Disallow consecutive comments
 318→
 319→Consecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
 320→- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
 321→- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
 322→- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
 323→
 324→Consecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
 325→
 326→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 327→
 328→    ```kotlin
 329→    // An EOL comment
 330→    // may be followed by another EOL comment
 331→    val foo = "foo"
 332→
 333→    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
 334→
 335→    /*
 336→     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
 337→     */
 338→
 339→    /**
 340→      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
 341→      */
 342→    fun bar() = "bar"
 343→    ```
 344→
 345→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 346→
 347→    ```kotlin
 348→    /*
 349→     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
 350→     */
 351→    /*
 352→     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
 353→     */
 354→    val bar = "bar" 
 355→
 356→    /**
 357→      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
 358→      */
 359→
 360→    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
 361→    ```
 362→
 363→Rule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
 364→
 365→!!! Note
 366→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 367→
 368→## Function literal
 369→
 370→Enforces the parameters of a function literal and the arrow to be written on the same line as the opening brace if the maximum line length is not exceeded. In case the parameters are wrapped to multiple lines then this is respected.
 371→
 372→If the function literal contains multiple parameter and at least one parameter other than the first parameter starts on a new line than all parameters and the arrow are wrapped to separate lines.
 373→
 374→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 375→
 376→    ```kotlin
 377→            val foobar1 = { foo + bar }
 378→            val foobar2 =
 379→                {
 380→                    foo + bar
 381→                }
 382→            val foobar3 =
 383→                { foo: Foo ->
 384→                    foo.repeat(2)
 385→                }
 386→            val foobar4 =
 387→                { foo: Foo, bar: Bar ->
 388→                    foo + bar
 389→                }
 390→            val foobar5 = { foo: Foo, bar: Bar -> foo + bar }
 391→            val foobar6 =
 392→                {
 393→                        foo: Foo,
 394→                        bar: Bar
 395→                    ->
 396→                    foo + bar
 397→                }
 398→
 399→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 400→    // at the X character on the right           X
 401→    val foobar7 =
 402→        barrrrrrrrrrrrrr {
 403→                fooooooooooooooo: Foo
 404→            ->
 405→            foo.repeat(2)
 406→        }
 407→    ```

 435→## Function signature
 436→
 437→Rewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. In case of function with a body expression, the body expression is placed on the same line as the function signature when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)
 438→
 439→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 440→
 441→    ```kotlin
 442→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 443→    // at the X character on the right           X
 444→    fun foooooooo(
 445→        a: Any,
 446→        b: Any,
 447→        c: Any
 448→    ): String {
 449→        // body
 450→    }
 451→
 452→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 453→    // at the X character on the right           X
 454→    fun bar(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
 455→        // body
 456→    }
 457→
 458→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
 459→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 460→    // at the X character on the right           X
 461→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 462→        .uppercase()
 463→
 464→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
 465→    // or 'always'.
 466→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 467→    // at the X character on the right           X
 468→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
 469→        "some-result"
 470→            .uppercase()
 471→    ```
 472→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 473→
 474→    ```kotlin
 475→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 476→    // at the X character on the right           X
 477→    fun foooooooo(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
 478→        // body
 479→    }
 480→
 481→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 482→    // at the X character on the right           X
 483→    fun bar(
 484→        a: Any,
 485→        b: Any,
 486→        c: Any
 487→    ): String {
 488→        // body
 489→    }
 490→
 491→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
 492→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 493→    // at the X character on the right           X
 494→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
 495→        "some-result"
 496→            .uppercase()
 497→
 498→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
 499→    // or 'always'.
 500→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 501→    // at the X character on the right           X
 502→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 503→        .uppercase()
 504→    ```
 505→
 506→Rule id: `function-signature` (`standard` rule set)

 508→## If else bracing
 509→
 510→If at least one branch of an if-else statement or an if-else-if statement is wrapped between curly braces then all branches should be wrapped between braces.
 511→
 512→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 513→
 514→    ```kotlin
 515→    fun foo(value: int) {
 516→        if (value > 0) {
 517→            doSomething()
 518→        } else if (value < 0) {
 519→            doSomethingElse()
 520→        } else {
 521→            doSomethingElse2()
 522→        }
 523→    }
 524→    ```
 525→
 526→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 527→
 528→    ```kotlin
 529→    fun foo(value: int) {
 530→        if (value > 0)
 531→            doSomething()
 532→        else if (value < 0) {
 533→            doSomethingElse()
 534→        } else
 535→            doSomethingElse2()
 536→    }
 537→    ```
 538→
 539→Rule id: `if-else-bracing` (`standard` rule set)
 540→
 541→!!! Note
 542→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 543→
 544→## If else wrapping
 545→
 546→A single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.
 547→
 548→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 549→
 550→    ```kotlin
 551→    fun foobar() {
 552→        if (true) foo()
 553→        if (true) foo() else bar()
 554→    }
 555→    ```
 556→
 557→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 558→
 559→    ```kotlin
 560→    fun foobar() {
 561→        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
 562→        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
 563→        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
 564→        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
 565→    }
 566→    ```
 567→
 568→Rule id: `if-else-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 569→
 570→!!! Note
 571→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.

 636→### Property naming
 637→
 638→Enforce naming of property.
 639→
 640→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 641→
 642→    ```kotlin
 643→    val FOO_1 = Foo()
 644→    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"

 646→    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
 647→
 648→    class Bar {
 649→        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
 650→        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
 651→
 652→        val foo2 = "foo"
 653→        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
 654→
 655→        // Backing property

 658→            get() = _elementList
 659→    }

 661→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 662→
 663→    ```kotlin
 664→    val Foo1 = Foo()
 665→    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
 666→
 667→    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
 668→
 669→    class Bar {
 670→        const val foo2 = "foo"
 671→        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
 672→
 673→        val FOO2 = "foo"
 674→        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
 675→
 676→        // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
 677→        private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
 678→
 679→        // Invalid backing property as '_elementList2' is not a private property
 680→        val _elementList2 = mutableListOf<Element>()

 683→    }

1022→## Wrapping
1023→
1024→### Content receiver wrapping
1025→
1026→Wraps the content receiver list to a separate line regardless of maximum line length. If the maximum line length is configured and is exceeded, wrap the context receivers and if needed its projection types to separate lines.
1027→
1028→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
1029→
1030→    ```kotlin
1031→    // ALways wrap regardless of whether max line length is set
1032→    context(Foo)
1033→    fun fooBar()
1034→
1035→    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
1036→    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line
1037→    context(
1038→        Fooooooooooooooooooo1,
1039→        Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2
1040→    )
1041→    fun fooBar()
1042→
1043→    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
1044→    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line.
1045→    // Also, wrap each of it projection types in case a context
1046→    // receiver does not fit on a single line after it has been
1047→    // wrapped.
1048→    context(
1049→        Foooooooooooooooo<
1050→            Foo,
1051→            Bar
1052→            >
1053→    )
1054→    fun fooBar()
1055→    ```
1056→
1057→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
1058→
1059→    ```kotlin
1060→    // Should be wrapped regardless of whether max line length is set
1061→    context(Foo) fun fooBar()
1062→
1063→    // Should be wrapped when the entire context receiver list does not
1064→    // fit on a single line
1065→    context(Fooooooooooooooooooo1, Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2)
1066→    fun fooBar()
1067→
1068→    // Should be wrapped when the entire context receiver list does not
1069→    // fit on a single line. Also, it should wrap each of it projection
1070→    // type in case a context receiver does not fit on a single line 
1071→    // after it has been wrapped.
1072→    context(Foooooooooooooooo<Foo, Bar>)
1073→    fun fooBar()
1074→    ```
1075→
1076→Rule id: `context-receiver-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
1077→
1078→### Enum wrapping
1079→
1080→An enum should be a single line, or each enum entry has to be placed on a separate line. In case the enumeration contains enum entries and declarations those are to be separated by a blank line.
1081→
1082→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
1083→
1084→    ```kotlin
1085→    enum class Foo { A, B, C, D }
1086→
1087→    enum class Foo {
1088→        A,
1089→        B,
1090→        C,
1091→        D,
1092→        ;
1093→
1094→        fun foo() = "foo"
1095→    }
1096→    ```
1097→
1098→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
1099→
1100→    ```kotlin
1101→    enum class Foo {
1102→        A,
1103→        B, C,
1104→        D
1105→    }
1106→
1107→    enum class Foo {
1108→        A;
1109→        fun foo() = "foo"
1110→    }
1111→    ```
1112→
1113→Rule id: `enum-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
1114→
1115→### Multiline expression wrapping
1116→
1117→Multiline expression on the right hand side of an expression are forced to start on a separate line. Expressions in return statement are excluded as that would result in a compilation error. 
1118→
1119→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
1120→
1121→    ```kotlin

1123→        foo(
1124→            parameterName =
1125→                "The quick brown fox "
1126→                    .plus("jumps ")
1127→                    .plus("over the lazy dog"),
1128→        )
1129→    ```
1130→
1131→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
1132→

1135→        parameterName = "The quick brown fox "
1136→            .plus("jumps ")
1137→            .plus("over the lazy dog"),

1143→!!! Note
1144→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
1145→
1146→### Statement wrapping
1147→

 31→/**

 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)

 72→    }

 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node

 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }

137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(

139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {

145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }

150→        }
151→    }

166→        }

 21→/**
 22→ * [Kotlin lang documentation](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#source-file-names):
 23→ * If a Kotlin file contains a single class (potentially with related top-level declarations), its name should be
 24→ * the same as the name of the class, with the `.kt` extension appended. If a file contains multiple classes,
 25→ * or only top-level declarations, choose a name describing what the file contains, and name the file accordingly.
 26→ * Use upper camel case with an uppercase first letter (also known as Pascal case),
 27→ * for example, `ProcessDeclarations.kt`.
 28→ *
 29→ * According to issue https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-21897/Kotlin-coding-convention-file-naming-for-class,
 30→ * "class" above should be read as any type of class (data class, enum class, sealed class) and interfaces.
 31→ *
 32→ * A strict implementation of guideline above had unwanted consequences:
 33→ *   - If the file contains a single top level private class, it does not make sense to force the name of file to be
 34→ *     identical to that class.
 35→ *   - If the file contains a coherent set of functions and one of those function returns an instance of a public class
 36→ *     which happens to be the only top level class in that file, it might not always be best to force the file to be
 37→ *     named after that class.
 38→ *   - Existing functionality regarding files containing a single top level object/typealias was lost.
 39→ *
 40→ * Exceptions to this rule:
 41→ * - file without `.kt` extension
 42→ * - file with name `package.kt`
 43→ */
 44→public class FilenameRule : StandardRule("filename") {
 45→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 46→        node: ASTNode,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49→    ) {
 50→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 51→            node as FileASTNode? ?: error("node is not ${FileASTNode::class} but ${node::class}")
 52→
 53→            val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
 54→            if (filePath?.endsWith(".kt") != true || filePath.endsWith("package.kt")) {
 55→                // ignore all non ".kt" files (including ".kts")
 56→                stopTraversalOfAST()
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            val fileName =
 61→                filePath
 62→                    .replace('\\', '/') // Ensure compatibility with Windows OS
 63→                    .substringAfterLast("/")
 64→                    .substringBefore(".")
 65→
 66→            val topLevelClassDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations(CLASS)
 67→            if (topLevelClassDeclarations.size == 1) {
 68→                val topLevelClassDeclaration = topLevelClassDeclarations.first()
 69→                if (node.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier)) {
 70→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // If the file only contains one (non-private) top level class and possibly some extension functions of
 73→                    // that class, then its filename should be identical to the class name.
 74→                    fileName.shouldMatchClassName(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier, emit)
 75→                }
 76→            } else {
 77→                val topLevelDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations()
 78→                if (topLevelDeclarations.size == 1) {
 79→                    val topLevelDeclaration = topLevelDeclarations.first()
 80→                    if (topLevelDeclaration.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ||
 81→                        topLevelDeclaration.elementType == TYPEALIAS
 82→                    ) {
 83→                        val pascalCaseIdentifier =
 84→                            topLevelDeclaration
 85→                                .identifier
 86→                                .toPascalCase()
 87→                        fileName.shouldMatchFileName(pascalCaseIdentifier, emit)
 88→                    } else {
 89→                        fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 90→                    }
 91→                } else {
 92→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 93→                }
 94→            }
 95→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 96→        }
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    private fun ASTNode.topLevelDeclarations(elementType: IElementType? = null): List<TopLevelDeclaration> =
100→        children()
101→            .filter { elementType == null || it.elementType == elementType }
102→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
103→            .mapNotNull { it.toTopLevelDeclaration() }
104→            .distinct()
105→            .toList()
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.doesNotHavePrivateModifier(): Boolean =
108→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
109→            ?.children()
110→            ?.none { it.text == "private" }
111→            ?: true
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(className: String) =
114→        children()
115→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
116→            .any { it.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() || it.isFunctionNotExtending(className) }
117→
118→    private fun ASTNode.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() = elementType in NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.isFunctionNotExtending(className: String) =
121→        elementType == FUN &&
122→            findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)?.text?.let { !it.contains(className) } ?: true
123→
124→    private fun String.shouldMatchClassName(
125→        className: String,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        if (this != className) {
129→            emit(
130→                0,
131→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single class and possibly also extension functions for that class and should be named same " +
132→                    "after that class '$className.kt'",
133→                false,
134→            )
135→        }
136→    }
137→
138→    private fun String.toPascalCase() = replaceFirstChar { it.uppercaseChar() }
139→
140→    private fun String.shouldMatchFileName(
141→        filename: String,
142→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
143→    ) {
144→        if (this != filename) {
145→            emit(
146→                0,
147→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single top level declaration and should be named '$filename.kt'",
148→                false,
149→            )
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun String.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit) {
154→        if (!this.matches(PASCAL_CASE_REGEX)) {
155→            emit(0, "File name '$this.kt' should conform PascalCase", false)
156→        }
157→    }
158→
159→    private data class TopLevelDeclaration(
160→        val elementType: IElementType,
161→        val identifier: String,
162→    )
163→
164→    private fun ASTNode.toTopLevelDeclaration(): TopLevelDeclaration? =
165→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
166→            ?.text
167→            ?.removeSurrounding("`")
168→            ?.let { TopLevelDeclaration(elementType, it) }
169→
170→    private companion object {
171→        val PASCAL_CASE_REGEX = "^[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*$".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
172→        val NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES = listOf(OBJECT_DECLARATION, TYPEALIAS, PROPERTY)
173→    }
174→}

636→### Property naming
637→
638→Enforce naming of property.
639→
640→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
641→
642→    ```kotlin
643→    val FOO_1 = Foo()
644→    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"
645→
646→    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
647→
648→    class Bar {
649→        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
650→        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
651→
652→        val foo2 = "foo"
653→        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
654→
655→        // Backing property
656→        private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
657→        val elementList: List<Element>
658→            get() = _elementList
659→    }
660→    ```
661→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
662→
663→    ```kotlin
664→    val Foo1 = Foo()
665→    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
666→
667→    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
668→
669→    class Bar {
670→        const val foo2 = "foo"
671→        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
672→
673→        val FOO2 = "foo"
674→        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
675→
676→        // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
677→        private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
678→
679→        // Invalid backing property as '_elementList2' is not a private property
680→        val _elementList2 = mutableListOf<Element>()
681→        val elementList2: List<Element>
682→            get() = _elementList2
683→    }
684→    ```
685→
686→!!! note
687→    Top level `val` properties and `const val` properties have to be written in screaming snake notation. Local `val` and `const val` are written in lower camel case.
688→
689→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PropertyName`.
690→
691→Rule id: `property-naming` (`standard` rule set)
692→
693→## No empty file
694→
695→A kotlin (script) file should not be empty. It needs to contain at least one declaration. Files only contain a package and/or import statements are as of that disallowed.

1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→### Added
  8→
  9→* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
 10→
 11→### Removed
 12→
 13→### Fixed
 14→
 15→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
 16→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
 17→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
 18→* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
 19→* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
 20→* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
 21→* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
 22→* Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
 23→* Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
 24→* Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
 25→* Fix null pointer exception for if-else statement with empty THEN block `if-else-bracing` [#2135](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2135)
 26→
 27→### Changed
 28→
 29→* Change default code style to `ktlint_official` ([#2143](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2143))
 30→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
 31→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
 32→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
 33→* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
 34→
 35→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
 36→
 37→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
 38→
 39→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
 40→
 41→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
 42→
 43→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
 44→
 45→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
 46→
 47→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
 48→
 49→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
 50→
 51→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
 52→
 53→* Replace `LeafElement.replaceWithText(String)` with `LeafElement.rawReplaceWithText(String)`.
 54→* Replace `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)` with `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)`. Note that this method inserts the new node (first) argument *before* the second argument node and as of that is not a simple replacement of the `PsiElement.addAfter(PsiElement, PsiElement)`.
 55→* Replace `PsiElement.replace(PsiElement)` with a sequence of `AstNode.addChild(AstNode, AstNode)` and `AstNode.removeChild(AstNode)`.
 56→
 57→Be aware that your custom rules might use other functions which also throw exceptions when the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is no longer supported.
 58→
 59→In order to help you to analyse and fix the problems with your custom rules, ktlint temporarily supports to disable the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` using a flag. This flag is available in the Ktlint CLI and in the `KtlintRuleEngine`. By default, the extension point is enabled like it was in previous versions of ktlint.
 60→
 61→At least you should analyse the problems by running your test suits by running ktlint and disabling the extension point. Next you can start with fixing and releasing the updated rules. All rules in this version of ktlint have already been refactored and are not dependent on the extension point anymore. In Ktlint CLI the flag is to be activated with parameter `--disable-kotlin-extension-point`. API Consumers that use the `KtlintRuleEngine` directly, have to set property `enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint` to `false`.
 62→
 63→At this point in time, it is not yet decided what the next steps will be. Ktlint might drop the support of the extension points entirely. Or, if the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` is fully supported at the time that ktlint will be based on kotlin 1.9 it might be kept. In either case, the flag will be dropped in a next version of ktlint.
 64→
 65→### Added
 66→
 67→* Add new experimental rule `binary-expression-wrapping`. This rule wraps a binary expression in case the max line length is exceeded ([#1940](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1940))
 68→* Add flag to disable extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` to analyse impact on custom rules [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981)

The changes look good. Let me summarize what I've done to fix this issue:

1. **Root Cause**: The `PropertyNamingRule` was treating all `val` properties in objects and at the top level as constants, requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming. However, according to the Android Kotlin style guide, mutable collection types (like `MutableMap`, `MutableList`, etc.) are NOT constants because their contents can be modified even if the reference is immutable.

2. **Fix Applied**:
   - Added imports for `TYPE_REFERENCE`, `USER_TYPE`, and `REFERENCE_EXPRESSION` element types
   - Modified the `visitProperty` function to check for mutable collection types before requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
   - Added a new `isMutableProperty()` function that checks if the property's declared type is a mutable collection type
   - Added a `MUTABLE_COLLECTION_TYPES` constant containing the names of common mutable collection types

3. **Tests Added**:
   - Added a new nested test class `Given mutable collection property` with multiple test cases:
     - Parameterized test for all supported mutable collection types in objects
     - Tests for companion objects, top-level properties, and invalid casing

4. **Documentation Updated**:
   - Updated `experimental.md` to include an example of mutable collection properties
   - Updated the note to clarify the exception for mutable collection types
   - Added an entry in `CHANGELOG.md`

The fix ensures that mutable collection properties in objects and at the top level are allowed to use camelCase naming instead of requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, while still enforcing proper naming conventions (the property still needs to be in valid camelCase format).

content.text:
The implementation is complete. Here's a summary of the changes made to fix the issue:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
Ktlint was requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for mutable collection property names in objects and at the top level, which violates the Android Kotlin style guide. According to the style guide, mutable collection types are NOT constants because their contents can be modified even if the reference is immutable (`val`).

### Solution

1. **Modified `PropertyNamingRule.kt`**:
   - Added imports for `TYPE_REFERENCE`, `USER_TYPE`, and `REFERENCE_EXPRESSION` element types
   - Added a new `isMutableProperty()` function that checks if the property's declared type is a mutable collection type
   - Modified the condition for treating properties as constants to exclude mutable collection types
   - Added a `MUTABLE_COLLECTION_TYPES` constant containing common mutable collection type names:
     - `MutableCollection`, `MutableList`, `MutableMap`, `MutableSet`
     - `ArrayList`, `ArrayDeque`, `HashSet`, `HashMap`, `LinkedHashMap`, `LinkedHashSet`

2. **Added tests in `PropertyNamingRuleTest.kt`**:
   - Parameterized tests for all supported mutable collection types in objects
   - Test for companion object properties with mutable types
   - Test for top-level properties with mutable types  
   - Test that invalid casing (not camelCase) on mutable collection properties still emits an error

3. **Updated documentation in `experimental.md`**:
   - Added example showing mutable collection properties using camelCase
   - Updated the note to explain the exception for mutable collection types

4. **Updated `CHANGELOG.md`**:
   - Added entry documenting the fix for mutable collection property naming

### Result
Now mutable collection properties like:
```kotlin
object Foo {
    val someMap: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = mutableMapOf()
}
```
Will be allowed with camelCase naming, while non-mutable properties will still require SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
```kotlin
object Foo {
    val SOME_STRING = "constant"
}
```

 31→/**
 32→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a property.
 33→ *
 34→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 35→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 36→ * indentation rule is not run.
 37→ *
 38→ * This rule has many similarities with the [ParameterWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 39→ */
 40→public class PropertyWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "property-wrapping",
 43→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 44→            setOf(
 45→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 46→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 47→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 48→            ),
 49→    ) {
 50→    private var line = 1
 51→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 52→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 53→
 54→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 55→        line = 1
 56→        indentConfig =
 57→            IndentConfig(
 58→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 59→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 60→            )
 61→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 65→        node: ASTNode,
 66→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 67→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 68→    ) {
 69→        if (node.elementType == PROPERTY) {
 70→            rearrangeProperty(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 71→        }
 72→    }
 73→
 74→    private fun rearrangeProperty(
 75→        node: ASTNode,
 76→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 77→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 78→    ) {
 79→        require(node.elementType == PROPERTY)
 80→
 81→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 82→
 83→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 84→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 85→        val fromNode =
 86→            node
 87→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 88→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 89→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 90→                ?: node
 91→
 92→        node
 93→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 94→            ?.let { colon ->
 95→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 96→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 97→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
 98→                    return
 99→                }
100→            }
101→
102→        node
103→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
104→            ?.let { typeReference ->
105→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference) > maxLineLength) {
106→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
107→                    return
108→                }
109→            }
110→
111→        node
112→            .findChildByType(EQ)
113→            ?.let { equal ->
114→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal) > maxLineLength) {
115→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
116→                    return
117→                }
118→            }
119→
120→        node
121→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
122→            ?.let { callExpression ->
123→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression) > maxLineLength) {
124→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
125→                    return
126→                }
127→            }
128→    }
129→
130→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
131→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
132→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
133→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
134→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
135→    }
136→
137→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
138→        node: ASTNode,
139→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
140→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
141→    ) {
142→        emit(
143→            node.startOffset - 1,
144→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
145→            true,
146→        )
147→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
148→        if (autoCorrect) {
149→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
154→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
155→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
156→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
157→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
158→    ) {
159→        emit(
160→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
161→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
162→            true,
163→        )
164→        LOGGER.trace {
165→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
166→        }
167→        if (autoCorrect) {
168→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
169→        }
170→    }
171→}

 21→/**
 22→ * [Kotlin lang documentation](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#source-file-names):
 23→ * If a Kotlin file contains a single class (potentially with related top-level declarations), its name should be
 24→ * the same as the name of the class, with the `.kt` extension appended. If a file contains multiple classes,
 25→ * or only top-level declarations, choose a name describing what the file contains, and name the file accordingly.
 26→ * Use upper camel case with an uppercase first letter (also known as Pascal case),
 27→ * for example, `ProcessDeclarations.kt`.
 28→ *
 29→ * According to issue https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-21897/Kotlin-coding-convention-file-naming-for-class,
 30→ * "class" above should be read as any type of class (data class, enum class, sealed class) and interfaces.
 31→ *
 32→ * A strict implementation of guideline above had unwanted consequences:
 33→ *   - If the file contains a single top level private class, it does not make sense to force the name of file to be
 34→ *     identical to that class.
 35→ *   - If the file contains a coherent set of functions and one of those function returns an instance of a public class
 36→ *     which happens to be the only top level class in that file, it might not always be best to force the file to be
 37→ *     named after that class.
 38→ *   - Existing functionality regarding files containing a single top level object/typealias was lost.
 39→ *
 40→ * Exceptions to this rule:
 41→ * - file without `.kt` extension
 42→ * - file with name `package.kt`
 43→ */
 44→public class FilenameRule : StandardRule("filename") {
 45→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 46→        node: ASTNode,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 49→    ) {
 50→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 51→            node as FileASTNode? ?: error("node is not ${FileASTNode::class} but ${node::class}")
 52→
 53→            val filePath = (node.psi as? KtFile)?.virtualFilePath
 54→            if (filePath?.endsWith(".kt") != true || filePath.endsWith("package.kt")) {
 55→                // ignore all non ".kt" files (including ".kts")
 56→                stopTraversalOfAST()
 57→                return
 58→            }
 59→
 60→            val fileName =
 61→                filePath
 62→                    .replace('\\', '/') // Ensure compatibility with Windows OS
 63→                    .substringAfterLast("/")
 64→                    .substringBefore(".")
 65→
 66→            val topLevelClassDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations(CLASS)
 67→            if (topLevelClassDeclarations.size == 1) {
 68→                val topLevelClassDeclaration = topLevelClassDeclarations.first()
 69→                if (node.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier)) {
 70→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 71→                } else {
 72→                    // If the file only contains one (non-private) top level class and possibly some extension functions of
 73→                    // that class, then its filename should be identical to the class name.
 74→                    fileName.shouldMatchClassName(topLevelClassDeclaration.identifier, emit)
 75→                }
 76→            } else {
 77→                val topLevelDeclarations = node.topLevelDeclarations()
 78→                if (topLevelDeclarations.size == 1) {
 79→                    val topLevelDeclaration = topLevelDeclarations.first()
 80→                    if (topLevelDeclaration.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ||
 81→                        topLevelDeclaration.elementType == TYPEALIAS
 82→                    ) {
 83→                        val pascalCaseIdentifier =
 84→                            topLevelDeclaration
 85→                                .identifier
 86→                                .toPascalCase()
 87→                        fileName.shouldMatchFileName(pascalCaseIdentifier, emit)
 88→                    } else {
 89→                        fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 90→                    }
 91→                } else {
 92→                    fileName.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit)
 93→                }
 94→            }
 95→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 96→        }
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    private fun ASTNode.topLevelDeclarations(elementType: IElementType? = null): List<TopLevelDeclaration> =
100→        children()
101→            .filter { elementType == null || it.elementType == elementType }
102→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
103→            .mapNotNull { it.toTopLevelDeclaration() }
104→            .distinct()
105→            .toList()
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.doesNotHavePrivateModifier(): Boolean =
108→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
109→            ?.children()
110→            ?.none { it.text == "private" }
111→            ?: true
112→
113→    private fun ASTNode.hasTopLevelDeclarationNotExtending(className: String) =
114→        children()
115→            .filter { it.doesNotHavePrivateModifier() }
116→            .any { it.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() || it.isFunctionNotExtending(className) }
117→
118→    private fun ASTNode.isNotClassRelatedTopLevelDeclaration() = elementType in NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.isFunctionNotExtending(className: String) =
121→        elementType == FUN &&
122→            findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)?.text?.let { !it.contains(className) } ?: true
123→
124→    private fun String.shouldMatchClassName(
125→        className: String,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        if (this != className) {
129→            emit(
130→                0,
131→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single class and possibly also extension functions for that class and should be named same " +
132→                    "after that class '$className.kt'",
133→                false,
134→            )
135→        }
136→    }
137→
138→    private fun String.toPascalCase() = replaceFirstChar { it.uppercaseChar() }
139→
140→    private fun String.shouldMatchFileName(
141→        filename: String,
142→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
143→    ) {
144→        if (this != filename) {
145→            emit(
146→                0,
147→                "File '$this.kt' contains a single top level declaration and should be named '$filename.kt'",
148→                false,
149→            )
150→        }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun String.shouldMatchPascalCase(emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit) {
154→        if (!this.matches(PASCAL_CASE_REGEX)) {
155→            emit(0, "File name '$this.kt' should conform PascalCase", false)
156→        }
157→    }
158→
159→    private data class TopLevelDeclaration(
160→        val elementType: IElementType,
161→        val identifier: String,
162→    )
163→
164→    private fun ASTNode.toTopLevelDeclaration(): TopLevelDeclaration? =
165→        findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
166→            ?.text
167→            ?.removeSurrounding("`")
168→            ?.let { TopLevelDeclaration(elementType, it) }
169→
170→    private companion object {
171→        val PASCAL_CASE_REGEX = "^[A-Z][A-Za-z\\d]*$".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
172→        val NON_CLASS_RELATED_TOP_LEVEL_DECLARATION_TYPES = listOf(OBJECT_DECLARATION, TYPEALIAS, PROPERTY)
173→    }
174→}
175→
176→public val FILENAME_RULE_ID: RuleId = FilenameRule().ruleId

 26→/**
 27→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 28→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 29→ */
 30→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 31→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 32→    Rule.Experimental {
 33→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 34→        node: ASTNode,
 35→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 36→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 37→    ) {
 38→        node
 39→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 40→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    private fun visitProperty(
 44→        property: ASTNode,
 45→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 46→    ) {
 47→        property
 48→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 49→            ?.let { identifier ->
 50→                when {
 51→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 52→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 53→                    }
 54→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 55→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 56→                    }
 57→                    property.hasConstModifier() -> {
 58→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 59→                    }
 60→                    (property.isTopLevelValue() || property.isObjectValue()) &&
 61→                        !property.isMutableProperty() -> {
 62→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 63→                    }
 64→                    else -> {
 65→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 66→                    }
 67→                }
 68→            }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 72→        identifier: ASTNode,
 73→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 74→    ) {
 75→        identifier
 76→            .text
 77→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 78→            ?.let {
 79→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 80→            }
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 84→        identifier: ASTNode,
 85→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 86→    ) {
 87→        identifier
 88→            .text
 89→            .takeUnless {
 90→                // Allow
 91→                // object Foo {
 92→                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 93→                // }
 94→                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 95→            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 96→            ?.let {
 97→                emit(
 98→                    identifier.startOffset,
 99→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
100→                    false,
101→                )
102→            }
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
106→        identifier: ASTNode,
107→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
108→    ) {
109→        identifier
110→            .text
111→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
112→            ?.let {
113→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
114→            }
115→    }
116→
117→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
118→
119→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
120→
121→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
122→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
123→            ?.children()
124→            .orEmpty()
125→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
126→
127→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
128→
129→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
130→
131→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
132→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
133→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
134→            containsValKeyword() &&
135→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
136→
137→    /**
138→     * A property is considered mutable if its type is a mutable collection type. A mutable collection property
139→     * should not be considered as constant even when it is declared as `val` since its contents can still be changed.
140→     * See: https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names
141→     */
142→    private fun ASTNode.isMutableProperty() =
143→        findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
144→            ?.findChildByType(USER_TYPE)
145→            ?.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)
146→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
147→            ?.text
148→            ?.let { it in MUTABLE_COLLECTION_TYPES }
149→            ?: false
150→
151→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
152→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
153→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
154→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
155→            ?.let { identifier ->
156→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
157→            }
158→            ?: false
159→
160→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
161→        treeParent
162→            .children()
163→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
164→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
165→            .any { it.text == identifier }
166→
167→    private companion object {
168→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
169→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
170→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
171→        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
172→        val MUTABLE_COLLECTION_TYPES =
173→            setOf(
174→                "MutableCollection",
175→                "MutableList",
176→                "MutableMap",
177→                "MutableSet",
178→                "ArrayList",
179→                "ArrayDeque",
180→                "HashSet",
181→                "HashMap",
182→                "LinkedHashMap",
183→                "LinkedHashSet",
184→            )
185→    }
186→}

In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)

 20→* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
 21→* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
